mission.title: rook: Support for arbitrary PersistentVolumes as backing storage
mission.description: Support for arbitrary PersistentVolumes as backing storage. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current rook deployment
steps.description:
Verify your rook version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
helm list -n rook 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working rook installation.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
To run more smoothly in various cloud providers, the OSDs should be able to run on top of arbitrary volumes. Persisted config folders for mons and osds should also use the same volumes, which is related to #796.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for arbitrary PersistentVolumes as backing storage
steps.description:
Description of your changes:  Update the Rook Roadmap as per community discussion
```yaml
sh-4.2# ceph osd tree
ID  CLASS WEIGHT  TYPE NAME                        STATUS REWEIGHT PRI-AFF
 -1       2.92676 root default
 -5       2.92676     region us-west-2

 -3       0.97559             host ip-10-0-140-53
  0   ssd 0.97559                 osd.0                up  1.00000 1.00000
-14       0.97559         zone us-west-2b
-13       0.97559             host ip-10-0-152-238
  1   ssd 0.97559                 osd.1                up  1.00000 1.00000
-10       0.97559         zone us-west-2c
 -9       0.97559             host ip-10-0-167-50
  2   
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for arbitrary PersistentVolumes as backing storage" is functioning correctly.
